regionalCenter.howToAccess: How to Access Regional Center Services
regionalCenter.step1Desc: Use the ZIP code finder above or check the list to find your Regional Center.
regionalCenter.step2Desc: Call your Regional Center to schedule an intake appointment and discuss eligibility.
regionalCenter.step3Desc: Complete an assessment to determine if you qualify for services (developmental disability must begin before age 18).
regionalCenter.step4Desc: Work with your service coordinator to create an Individualized Program Plan (IPP).
regionalCenter.step5Desc: Select providers from our map that accept your Regional Center's funding.

faq.title: Frequently Asked Questions
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about ABA therapy, Regional Centers, and finding resources in Los Angeles County
faq.aba1A:
Applied Behavior Analysis (ABA) is a scientific, evidence-based therapy that helps improve specific behaviors, such as social skills, communication, reading, and academics, as well as adaptive learning skills.

ABA therapy is particularly effective for individuals with autism spectrum disorder (ASD) and developmental disabilities. It uses positive reinforcement and data-driven techniques to teach new skills and reduce challenging behaviors.
faq.aba2Q: Who can benefit from ABA therapy?
faq.aba2A:
ABA therapy can benefit individuals of all ages with:

  • Autism Spectrum Disorder (ASD) - Most common use case
  • Developmental Disabilities - Cognitive or intellectual delays
  • Learning Disabilities - Academic challenges
  • Behavioral Disorders - ADHD, ODD, and others
  • Communication Disorders - Speech and language delays

While ABA is most commonly used for children with autism, it can be beneficial for anyone needing structured behavioral intervention.
faq.aba3Q: How long does ABA therapy take?
faq.aba3A:
ABA therapy duration varies based on individual needs:

  • Intensive programs: 20-40 hours per week (common for young children with autism)
  • Focused programs: 10-20 hours per week (specific skill development)
  • Consultation: 1-5 hours per week (maintenance or specific issues)

Timeline: Most children participate for 1-3 years, but some may need longer or shorter periods depending on their progress and goals.
faq.rc1A:
Regional Centers are nonprofit agencies funded by the State of California that provide services and support to individuals with developmental disabilities.

They serve as the main point of contact for accessing state-funded services including ABA therapy, respite care, day programs, and more.
faq.rc2Q: How do I find my Regional Center?
faq.rc2A:
Your Regional Center is determined by your ZIP code. Use our Regional Centers page or enter your ZIP code in the map to find which one serves your area.

Los Angeles County is served by 7 Regional Centers, each covering specific geographic areas.
faq.rc3A: Yes! Services through Regional Centers are free for eligible individuals with developmental disabilities. Eligibility is based on having a qualifying developmental disability that began before age 18.
faq.ins1A:
Many insurance plans cover ABA therapy, especially for autism. Coverage varies by plan:

  • Private Insurance: California law requires coverage for autism treatment
  • Medi-Cal: Covers ABA therapy for eligible individuals
  • Regional Centers: May fund services not covered by insurance
faq.ins2Q: What if I don't have insurance?
faq.ins2A:
If you don't have insurance, you still have options:

  • Apply for Medi-Cal (California's Medicaid program)
  • Contact your Regional Center for state-funded services
  • Ask resources about sliding scale fees
  • Check for grant programs and scholarships
faq.map1Q: How do I use the KINDD map?
faq.map1A:
Using the map is easy:

  1. Enter your ZIP code or allow location access
  2. Browse resources in your area
  3. Use filters to narrow by therapy type, insurance, etc.
  4. Click on a resource for details and contact info
faq.map2A: Our resources are verified through Regional Center vendor lists and direct outreach. We update information regularly, but always recommend calling to confirm details before your first visit.

about.title: About KINDD Provider Map
about.subtitle: Helping families in Los Angeles County find ABA therapy and developmental disability services
about.missionText: We create hope and build healthier futures by connecting families with the autism and developmental disability services they need.
about.missionDescription: KINDD (Kids in Need of Developmental Disabilities) is a free, interactive map developed by Children's Hospital Los Angeles to help families find ABA therapy resources and autism services across Los Angeles County.
about.interactiveMapDesc: Visualize resource locations geographically and see your Regional Center boundaries.
about.locationSearchDesc: Automatically find resources near you based on your ZIP code or current location.
about.smartFiltersDesc: Search by insurance accepted, therapy types, age groups, and diagnoses treated.
about.rcIntegrationDesc: Understand which Regional Center serves your area and find their resources.
about.quickContactDesc: Get phone numbers, websites, and directions with one click.
about.mobileFriendlyDesc: Works seamlessly on phones, tablets, and desktops for search on-the-go.
about.rcExplanation: Regional Centers are nonprofit agencies funded by the State of California to provide services and support to individuals with developmental disabilities. They are the gateway to free or low-cost services for eligible families.
about.step1Desc: Allow location access or enter your ZIP code to find resources near you.
about.step2Desc: The map automatically shows which Regional Center serves your area.
about.step3Desc: Use filters to narrow results by insurance, therapy types, and more.
about.step4Desc: View resource details, get directions, and contact them directly.
about.whoWeServeText: KINDD helps families seeking services for children and adults with:
about.chlaDescription: Children's Hospital Los Angeles (CHLA) has been a leader in pediatric healthcare for over 120 years. As one of the top children's hospitals in the nation, CHLA is committed to creating hope and building healthier futures for children and families.
about.freeToUse: Free to Use, No Registration Required
about.freeToUseText: KINDD is completely free to use. There are no fees, subscriptions, or registration required. We don't sell your data or charge providers to be listed. Our goal is simply to help families find the services they need.
about.startSearch: Start your search now - it only takes a few seconds.
